soil_type: Clay loam to silty loam soil with good water-holding capacity
water_requirement: High (standing water during early growth stages)
climate: Warm and humid climate with temperature range 20–35 °C
notes: Rice is the staple food crop of Nepal and contributes significantly to national food security. It is mainly cultivated during the monsoon season under rain-fed and irrigated conditions, particularly in the Terai and lower hill regions.

soil_type: Well-drained loam to clay loam soil
notes: Wheat is Nepal’s second most important staple crop after rice. It is mainly cultivated in winter under irrigated and rain-fed conditions, especially in Terai and mid-hill regions.

soil_type: Well-drained loam to sandy-loam soil with good organic matter
notes: Maize is one of the most important cereal crops of Nepal. It is a staple food in hill regions and a major commercial crop in Terai. In mountain areas, maize cultivation is limited to lower elevations due to temperature constraints
